proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_051 is a 3-level ordinal performance-status-like variable; higher levels of feature_051 are associated with a lower probability of objective_response.

analyses.code: from scipy.stats import chi2_contingency; chi2_contingency(pd.crosstab(df['feature_051'], df['objective_response']))
analyses.result_summary: Response rate falls monotonically across feature_051 levels: level 0 = 21.08% (n=17,557), level 1 = 15.70% (n=24,962), level 2 = 11.05% (n=7,481). Chi-square test highly significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_011 (continuous count, range 0-28) is inversely associated with objective_response; patients with higher feature_011 values have a lower probability of response.

analyses.code: from scipy.stats import ttest_ind; ttest_ind(df.loc[y==1,'feature_011'], df.loc[y==0,'feature_011'], equal_var=False); smf.logit('y ~ feature_011', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Mean feature_011 = 3.38 in responders vs 3.93 in non-responders. Univariable logistic OR per unit = 0.962 (p=1.8e-32). Binned response rates show monotone decline: 19.1% (0-1) -> 17.0% (2-3) -> 17.0% (4-5) -> 15.1% (6-10) -> 12.8% (11+).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_013 (binary) is a negative prognostic marker: presence (=1) is associated with a lower probability of objective_response than absence (=0).
feature_067 (binary) is a negative prognostic marker: presence (=1) is associated with a lower probability of objective_response than absence (=0).

analyses.code: pd.crosstab(df['feature_013'], df['objective_response']); chi2_contingency(...)
analyses.result_summary: Response rate 19.6% when feature_013=0 vs 15.4% when feature_013=1. Univariable logistic OR=0.74.

analyses.code: chi2_contingency(pd.crosstab(df['feature_067'], df['objective_response']))
analyses.result_summary: Response rate 17.7% when feature_067=0 vs 14.4% when feature_067=1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_006 (binary) is a positive predictor: presence (=1) is associated with a higher probability of objective_response than absence (=0).
feature_007 (binary) is a positive predictor: presence (=1) is associated with a higher probability of objective_response than absence (=0).
feature_039 (binary) is a positive predictor: presence (=1) is associated with a higher probability of objective_response than absence (=0).

analyses.code: chi2_contingency(pd.crosstab(df['feature_006'], df['objective_response']))
analyses.result_summary: Response rate 16.4% when feature_006=0 vs 18.2% when feature_006=1.

analyses.code: chi2_contingency(pd.crosstab(df['feature_007'], df['objective_response']))
analyses.result_summary: Response rate 16.4% when feature_007=0 vs 17.5% when feature_007=1.

analyses.code: chi2_contingency(pd.crosstab(df['feature_039'], df['objective_response']))
analyses.result_summary: Response rate 16.4% when feature_039=0 vs 17.4% when feature_039=1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_099 (continuous, range 1.7-5.5; likely a serum protein such as albumin) is positively associated with objective_response: higher feature_099 values are associated with a higher probability of response.

feature_063 (continuous, range 0.03-297; likely an inflammatory or hepatic lab) is inversely associated with objective_response: higher feature_063 values are associated with a lower probability of response.

analyses.code: ttest_ind(df.loc[y==1,'feature_099'], df.loc[y==0,'feature_099'])
analyses.result_summary: Mean feature_099 = 3.823 in responders vs 3.800 in non-responders. Small but significant positive effect.

analyses.code: ttest_ind(df.loc[y==1,'feature_063'], df.loc[y==0,'feature_063'])
analyses.result_summary: Mean feature_063 = 5.78 in responders vs 6.15 in non-responders. Inverse association confirmed.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After mutual adjustment in a multivariable logistic regression that includes feature_051, feature_011, feature_013, feature_067, feature_006, feature_007, feature_039, feature_099, feature_063, and demographics, feature_051 will remain the strongest single predictor (largest absolute z-statistic) of objective_response with a negative coefficient.

analyses.code: smf.logit('y ~ feature_051 + feature_011 + feature_013 + feature_067 + feature_006 + feature_007 + feature_039 + feature_099 + feature_063 + feature_092 + feature_076 + feature_112 + feature_078 + C(feature_123) + C(feature_043) + C(feature_057) + C(feature_005)', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Multivariable logistic on all 50,000 patients: feature_051 coef = -0.381 (z=-20.75, OR=0.683), the largest |z| of any predictor; pseudo-R^2 = 0.020; LLR p<1e-177. All eight univariably-significant features remained significant after mutual adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patient age (feature_078, range 30-90) has no clinically meaningful main effect on objective_response after adjusting for feature_051 and other prognostic features.

analyses.code: smf.logit('y ~ feature_078 + feature_051 + feature_011 + feature_013 + feature_067 + feature_006', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted age coefficient = 0.003 per SD (z=0.28, p=0.78). No detectable independent effect of age on response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Tumor histology (feature_043: squamous vs adenocarcinoma) does not have a clinically meaningful adjusted effect on objective_response; squamous histology shows a small non-significant trend toward higher response after adjustment.

analyses.code: df['hist_squam'] = (df['feature_043']=='squamous').astype(int); smf.logit('y ~ hist_squam + feature_051 + feature_011 + feature_013 + feature_067 + feature_006', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Crude response rate 17.4% in squamous vs 16.7% in adenocarcinoma. Adjusted OR = 1.04 (p=0.15). No significant histology effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Smoking status (feature_057: current/former/never) has at most a small effect on objective_response; current smokers will have the highest response rate and never-smokers the lowest, consistent with patterns expected for immune-checkpoint-inhibitor-treated NSCLC.

analyses.code: df.groupby('feature_057')['objective_response'].agg(['mean','count']); smf.logit with smk_never + smk_current and full covariate adjustment
analyses.result_summary: Crude rates: current 17.23%, former 16.85%, never 16.38%. Direction matches hypothesis but adjusted ORs essentially null after controlling for biomarkers (smk_never adj OR=0.99 p=0.86; smk_current adj OR=1.00 p=0.91).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Race (feature_123 with five levels: white, hispanic, black, asian, other) shows small unadjusted differences in objective_response; non-white patients have a slightly higher adjusted response rate than white patients.

analyses.code: df.groupby('feature_123')['y'].mean(); adj logistic with race indicators against white reference
analyses.result_summary: Crude rates: other 18.5%, black 17.8%, asian 17.7%, hispanic 17.2%, white 16.6%. After adjusting for top 7 covariates: race_black OR=1.08 (p=0.031), race_hispanic OR=1.04 (p=0.29), race_asian OR=1.08 (p=0.13), race_other OR=1.13 (p=0.14). Combined non-white-vs-white OR=1.07 (p=0.011).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Insurance status (feature_005: medicaid/medicare/private/uninsured) shows a small adjusted gradient: patients with private insurance have a higher probability of objective_response than patients on medicaid (the reference, lowest crude response).

analyses.code: df.groupby('feature_005')['y'].mean(); adj logistic vs medicaid reference
analyses.result_summary: Crude rates: private 17.20%, uninsured 17.00%, medicare 16.85%, medicaid 16.13%. Adjusted ORs (vs medicaid) after full covariate adjustment: medicare OR=1.07 (p=0.10), private OR=1.09 (p=0.019), uninsured OR=1.09 (p=0.23). Private insurance shows a small but significant advantage; pattern is not monotone with expected socioeconomic gradient (uninsured roughly equal to medicare/private).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative effect of feature_013 on objective_response is the same across the five race categories of feature_123 (no race-by-treatment-marker effect heterogeneity).

analyses.code: Likelihood-ratio test: y ~ feature_013 * (race_black + race_hisp + race_asian + race_other) vs y ~ feature_013 + race indicators
analyses.result_summary: LR statistic = 3.17 on 4 df, p=0.53. No evidence that the feature_013 effect varies by race; individual interaction terms all p>0.13.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative effect of feature_013 on objective_response is the same across the four insurance categories of feature_005 (no insurance-by-treatment-marker effect heterogeneity).

analyses.code: LR test: y ~ feature_013 * (ins_med + ins_pri + ins_unins) vs y ~ feature_013 + insurance indicators
analyses.result_summary: LR statistic = 2.11 on 3 df, p=0.55. Individual interactions all p>0.45. No evidence the feature_013 association varies by insurance status.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is no interaction between feature_011 and feature_051 (performance-status-like): the marginal slope of feature_011 on log-odds of objective_response is the same in each performance-status stratum.

There is no significant interaction between any of the eight top binary features (feature_013, feature_067, feature_006, feature_007, feature_039, feature_076, feature_112) and feature_051 (performance-status-like).

analyses.code: smf.logit('y ~ feature_011 * feature_051', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef = -0.0034 (p=0.50). No evidence of effect modification.

analyses.code: for each top binary feature: smf.logit('y ~ feature_X * feature_051', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Smallest interaction p-value across the 8 features tested was p=0.10 (feature_076). All other p>0.13. No reliable evidence of effect modification by performance status.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_006 and feature_039 have a synergistic (super-additive on the log-odds scale) interaction: when both are present (=1) the response rate is greater than predicted by the additive sum of their individual effects.
feature_007 and feature_039 are similarly synergistic on objective_response.
feature_006 and feature_007 are similarly synergistic on objective_response.

analyses.code: smf.logit('y ~ feature_006 * feature_039', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient = +0.184 (OR_int=1.20, p=0.00042). Co-presence of feature_006=1 and feature_039=1 increases response above additive expectation.

analyses.code: smf.logit('y ~ feature_007 * feature_039', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient = +0.146 (OR_int=1.16, p=0.0024).

analyses.code: smf.logit('y ~ feature_006 * feature_007', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient = +0.149 (OR_int=1.16, p=0.0043).

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a positive three-way interaction among feature_006, feature_007, and feature_039 on objective_response: patients with all three markers present (=1) have a response rate substantially higher than predicted by the sum of pairwise effects.

analyses.code: smf.logit('y ~ feature_006 * feature_007 * feature_039', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Three-way interaction coefficient = +0.391 (p=1.8e-4). Observed response rates by (feature_006, feature_007, feature_039): triple-negative (0,0,0)=16.1%, all eight other cells 16.2-16.9%, EXCEPT triple-positive (1,1,1)=22.98% (n=3,216) — a striking jump consistent with combinatorial biomarker selection.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_092 (continuous, range 0-0.798, with ~10% mass at exactly 0) has a non-monotone relationship with objective_response, with elevated response at zero, a dip at intermediate values, and a sharp rise at high values (>=0.5) — a pattern consistent with PD-L1-tumor-proportion-score-like biomarker behavior.

analyses.result_summary: Response rate by feature_092 bin: =0 -> 17.30% (n=5,272); (0,0.10] -> 16.56% (n=5,514); (0.10,0.25] -> 16.40% (n=10,496); (0.25,0.50] -> 16.37% (n=17,969); (0.50,1.0] -> 18.23% (n=10,749). Dichotomization at 0.5 yields OR=1.13 (p=2.9e-5). Pattern is U-shaped (dip in middle) consistent with mixed populations + a high-expression responder subset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive effect of feature_006 on objective_response is amplified when feature_092 is high (>=0.5), consistent with feature_006 acting as a treatment whose benefit depends on a biomarker captured by feature_092.

analyses.code: smf.logit('y ~ feature_092 * feature_006', df).fit(); stratified rates
analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient (continuous feature_092 * feature_006) = +0.594 (p=1.2e-6). Stratified: when feature_092<0.5, response 16.4% (feature_006=0) vs 17.0% (feature_006=1), OR=1.05 (p=0.14). When feature_092>=0.5, response 16.5% (feature_006=0) vs 22.5% (feature_006=1), OR=1.47 (p=1.8e-13). Strong qualitative effect modification.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Continuous feature_092 also positively interacts with feature_007 and feature_039 (the same triad that showed positive 3-way interaction in iteration 16), consistent with feature_092 capturing a shared biomarker for which feature_006/007/039 are co-acting treatments.

analyses.code: for b in [feature_007, feature_039]: smf.logit('y ~ feature_092 * b', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_092 x feature_007 interaction coef = +0.606 (p=7.9e-8); feature_092 x feature_039 interaction coef = +0.591 (p=1.6e-7). Both highly significant and direction-consistent with the feature_006 finding.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A composite biomarker-positive subgroup defined as feature_092>=0.5 AND feature_006=1 has a substantially higher objective_response rate than all other patients combined.

analyses.code: df['mark']=((df['feature_092']>=0.5)&(df['feature_006']==1)).astype(int); smf.logit('y ~ mark', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Composite-positive subgroup (n=3,150, 6.3% of cohort): response rate 22.51%. All others (n=46,850): response rate 16.52%. OR=1.47, p=5.6e-18.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the 77 binary features tested, feature_006 has a significant negative interaction with feature_027 on objective_response (i.e., the positive effect of feature_006 is attenuated when feature_027 is also present).

analyses.code: for c in binary_cols: smf.logit('y ~ feature_006 * c', df).fit()  # 77 tests; second-strongest finding
analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient feature_006:feature_027 = -0.266 (p=4.3e-4). Negative interaction is the strongest negative pairwise interaction with feature_006. Note this is one of 77 tests but the magnitude survives even a Bonferroni correction at alpha=0.05/77=6.5e-4.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the 77 binary features tested, feature_013 has a significant negative interaction with feature_034 on objective_response.

analyses.code: for c in binary_cols: smf.logit('y ~ feature_013 * c', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient feature_013:feature_034 = -0.215 (p=0.0078). Strongest pairwise interaction with feature_013 across the 77-feature scan, but does NOT survive Bonferroni correction (0.05/77 = 6.5e-4); should be regarded as suggestive only.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_092 has a stronger positive association with objective_response in adenocarcinoma (feature_043='adenocarcinoma') than in squamous (feature_043='squamous') tumors.

analyses.code: for h in ['adenocarcinoma','squamous']: smf.logit('y ~ feature_092', df[df['feature_043']==h]).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Stratified slopes: adenocarcinoma coef=0.162 (p=0.015, n=36,099); squamous coef=0.173 (p=0.10, n=13,901). Magnitudes are similar; the squamous estimate is non-significant largely due to smaller sample. No clear histology-by-feature_092 interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a final refined multivariable logistic model that includes feature_051, feature_011, feature_013, feature_067, feature_006, feature_007, feature_039, feature_099, feature_063, feature_092, feature_076, feature_112, plus histology, smoking, insurance and race indicators, all 12 of the originally-identified biomarker features remain independently significant (p<0.05), while smoking and histology do not.

analyses.code: smf.logit('y ~ feature_051 + feature_011 + feature_013 + feature_067 + feature_006 + feature_007 + feature_039 + feature_099 + feature_063 + feature_092 + feature_076 + feature_112 + hist_squam + smk_current + smk_never + ins_pri + race_nonwhite', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Final model: all 12 biomarker features p<0.05 (most p<0.001). race_nonwhite OR=1.066 (p=0.011); ins_pri OR=1.040 (p=0.111); hist_squam OR=1.041 (p=0.154); smk_current OR=0.987 (p=0.66); smk_never OR=1.001 (p=0.97). Pseudo-R^2=0.020; LLR p<5e-184. Demographic and smoking variables explain little additional variance once biomarker features are in the model.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Despite a very large sample (n=50,000) the absolute predictive performance of objective_response from the available features is modest: McFadden pseudo-R^2 < 0.05, indicating substantial residual outcome heterogeneity not explained by any combination of the measured features.

analyses.code: Final 17-feature multivariable logistic; pseudo-R^2 = 1 - LL_full/LL_null
analyses.result_summary: Final-model pseudo-R^2 = 0.0202; LL_full=-22,252 vs LL_null=-22,710. Cohort-wide response rate is 16.89% (8,447/50,000); even the best-defined biomarker-positive subgroup (feature_092>=0.5 AND feature_006=1) reaches only 22.5%, far from a clinically high response rate. Conclusion: features identified are statistically robust prognostic/predictive signals (driven by performance-status-like feature_051, tumor-burden-like feature_011, biomarker-treatment combinations involving feature_006/007/039/092, and inverse markers feature_013/067/063), but most of the variance in response remains unexplained.
